_comment_what: Stryker mutation testing config. Stryker injects small bugs ('mutants') into the source one at a time and re-runs the tests; a 'killed' mutant is one that some test caught, a 'survived' mutant is a real gap (covered code that no assertion actually verifies). See https://stryker-mutator.io.
_comment_scope: Scoped to one source file at a time — repoint `mutate` + `disableTypeChecks` and update vitest.stryker.config.ts when running for a different service. Narrowing test discovery via the vitest config keeps Stryker from re-running the full suite per mutant.
_comment_run: From packages/sdk: ./node_modules/.bin/stryker run — then open reports/mutation/mutation.html for the per-mutant view.
_comment_ci: Not wired into CI. One-shot signal that goes beyond branch coverage: high mutation score = tests actually verify behavior, not just touch lines.
